title: Super Simple Pocket-Hole Bed Frame (Queen)
summary: A pocket-holed headboard and footboard with a plywood-backed headboard panel, joined by 2x6 siderails — sized here for queen, with twin and full dimensions available.
description:
This bed keeps the headboard and footboard as flat, simple frames rather than planked panels — a pair of 2x4 legs and horizontal boards, pocket-screwed together, with a single plywood sheet screwed to the back of the headboard for a smooth, painted-panel look instead of individual board joints. That plywood back is also what keeps a tall headboard frame from racking, doing the same job a plank panel does in this catalog's other bed frames, just with one part instead of many.

Build the headboard and footboard complete and prefinished — paint or stain them along with the siderails before final assembly, since the inside faces of a bed frame this simple are much easier to reach flat on a bench than after everything is screwed together in the bedroom.

The siderails attach directly to the headboard and footboard with pocket screws at a fixed 13-1/2" height (matching the footboard height), assembled in the room rather than the shop, since a queen frame this size is awkward to move once joined. Queen and full both want a center inner cleat — full and up can also add a center leg under it for real weight support, which matters more for a contract-grade or rental bed that will see less careful use than a personal one.

steps.title: Cut all the parts
steps.body: Cut two headboard legs at 50", two headboard top and bottom at 56", two footboard legs at 13-1/2" and two inner cleats at 80" from the 2x4, 8 ft or stud length. Cut one footboard main board at 56" and two siderails at 80" from the 2x6, 8 ft or stud length. Cut one headboard back panel at 40-3/4" from the 1/4" to 1/2" plywood, 61" x 40-3/4". Cut twelve bed slats at 60" from the 1x4, 10 ft (or 1x3, 10 ft). Allowing about 1/8" for each saw cut, that comes to six 8 ft 2x4, 8 ft or stud length, three 8 ft 2x6, 8 ft or stud length and twelve 10 ft 1x4, 10 ft (or 1x3, 10 ft). Square every cut and label each piece as it comes off the saw.

steps.title: Build the headboard and footboard frames
steps.body: Drill two 1-1/2" pocket holes on each end of the horizontal boards and attach to the legs to build the headboard and footboard frames.

steps.title: Attach the headboard back panel
steps.body: Attach the plywood back panel to the headboard frame with screws, nails, or staples and glue.

steps.title: Prefinish and join the siderails
steps.body: Drill three 1-1/2" pocket holes on each end of the 2x6 siderails. Paint or stain the headboard, footboard, and siderails now, while they're easy to reach. In the room, attach the siderails to the headboard and footboard with 2-1/2" pocket hole screws, at a 13-1/2" attachment height matching the footboard.

steps.title: Add the inner cleats
steps.body: Add the inner cleats along the siderails. A center cleat is recommended for queen and full size. For contract-grade use or extra strength, a center leg can be added under the center cleat.

steps.title: Slat and finish
steps.body: Place the slats about 4" apart on the cleats and attach with the 1-1/4" fasteners.

images.alt: Simple queen bed frame with a plywood-backed headboard and 2x6 siderails
